text: A COUPLE ABOUT TO BE MARRIED DISCUSS IF IT IS TOO RISKY TO BRING HIS DEMENTED MOTHER
TO THEIR WEDDING. COULD UPDATING HER SURVEILLANCE EQUIPMENT IMPROVE THEIR LIVES?
Anna's son, Miilu, is discussing with
his fiancé if Anna should attend their
wedding or not. Anna suffers from
hallucinatory dementia but is already a
user at e-Na, a helping equipment for
An update will enable them to help her
occur and will make it possible
to have her at their wedding.
So why shouldn't Miilu update Anna's
Editor & Colorist: Wiebe van der Vliet (NL)

Award winning director Nivi Pedersen (b.1989) is a
Greenlandic filmmaker based in Nuuk, Greenland.
Wanting to portray different aspects of Greenland
and the Greenlandic culture for both a national and
an international audience she started Nivaara Films
in 2017 and her filmography includes both
documentary and fiction filmmaking as well as
The concept to UPDATED came from Ulannaq Ingemann and came from an urge to mobilize the
idea of what Greenlandic filmmaking is as well as exploring the genre of sci-fi dystopia. It is no
secret we were very inspired by watching "Black Mirror" on Netflix in 2017 and I delved into the
project loving the idea of "what could that look like in a Greenlandic context?"
The original concept consisted of 3 short films by different writers and directors and one of the
ideas was about a woman with dementia and a wedding.
Unfortunately dementia is not a concept unknown to me and my family and the character "Anna"
consists of traits from both my grandmother and my grandfathers sister. Their dementia affected
them each in two completely different ways and "getting to know them" better through the writing
process and seeing Vivi Nielsen play this role so empathetically was a strange privilege I didn't know
The knowledge of the extensive care needed, what toll it takes on the family members and the
powerlessness of not being able to be there in person all the time was what created the helping
surveillance equipment: "e-Na". And that is when the dilemma between caring for a loved one, privacy
violation and finally potentially altering chemical (im)balances in the brain arose.
Anna sits inside a lot in this film and we as a viewer can see the irresistible nature only through
the windows. Nature has always been an extra character in all films from and about Greenland and
there is nothing wrong or unnatural with that. But not having many exterior scenes has a twofold
motivation in wanting to underline Annas feelings and a curiosity to make and see what a film from
Greenland might look like when having minimal amount of exterior scenes.
